repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1: finite joint-completion LP sharpness; pid_dynamic_iv_compliance/v2: finite compliance-mixture logic without network exposure geometry

published_prior_art.cite: hoshino_yanagi_2024_noncompliance_unknown_interference: identifies and estimates complier effects with unknown interference; schroder_oprescu_feuerriegel_kallus_2026_networks_partialid: sharp bounds for treatment exposure-map misspecification

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1 has one treatment-exposure shared-completion gap but no IV compliance or second exposure map

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1 has a hidden-edge compatibility witness but no completion-principal-strata deficit

published_prior_art.cite: acerenza_martinez_iriarte_sanchez_becerra_spini_2025_household_bounds gives finite two-person encouragement bounds, not hidden-network dual-map compatibility certificates

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The strict open-class product-relaxation gap has no valid concrete witness because Exhibit 9.1 is internally inconsistent; tier=field below novelty_target=flagship until a correct non-corner witness is supplied.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The target weights are not well pinned: T_{s_D,s_Z} and q_{i,s_D,s_Z,r_D,r_Z} depend on the latent take-up table but omit it, and N_c(s_Z;G) does not normalize the same (s_D,s_Z)-conditioned weights used in the numerator.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The claimed finite mixed-integer linear program skips the variable complier denominator and binary-takeup-by-outcome products; as written the endpoint problem is not simply a linear MILP over the displayed set.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Exhibit 9.1 fails: with hidden edges only to unit 4 and Z_4 fixed at 0, leaves 1 and 2 cannot have r_Z=1, so the claimed product value 1 and shared value 1/2 do not follow.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The statement switches from Delta_{IVX,u}(P) to Delta_{IVX}(P) and invokes valid inequalities of a projected 'polytope' even though the displayed feasible object is a mixed-integer union unless a convex-hull formulation is explicitly defined.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace Exhibit 9.1 with a fully enumerated four-unit or five-unit witness where the encouraged hidden neighbor can actually generate r_Z=1, and compute both shared and product endpoints from the observable law.
Redefine T, q, qbar, and N_c with explicit dependence on the latent take-up table and make the numerator and denominator weights match the intended complier target cell.
State the endpoint program as either a mixed-integer fractional program with a named linearization, or as a convex-hull polytope with explicit auxiliary variables and constraints.
Add location-level anchors for the closest comparator claims, especially the exact Hoshino-Yanagi and Schroder et al. sections that leave the dual-map finite sharpness problem open.

recommended_next_step: Redraft only after repairing the target normalization and supplying a valid compatibility-deficit witness; otherwise pivot away from this flagship upgrade.

upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The proposal genuinely widens the parent from treatment-equals-assignment shared completion to randomized encouragement with monotone noncompliance and a second exposure map, but the current flagship witness is unsound.
